description: Lift-and-shift Contoso's claims adjudication stack from colo VMs to Azure with minimal logic change: preserve weekend batch windows, queued workers, and file-based carrier integrations while adopting managed SQL and private networking.
constraints: Carrier SFTP endpoints must see unchanged source IPv4 allow-list during phase 1
Restore RPO under 1 hour for the transactional database
No public RDP or SSH listeners on application subnets
requiredCapabilities: Managed secrets rotation aligned with carrier re-credentialing cadence
Autoscale worker tier for seasonal claim volume
assumptions: Application binaries are containerizable without framework downgrades
Test data sets exclude production PII
inlineRequirements: Call out phased DNS migration and explicit rollback criteria for the cutover weekend

documents.name: fictional-carrier-integration-notes.txt
documents.content: Fictional notes: two primary carriers, hourly pull windows, max file size constraints.

topologyHints: Place integration workers closest to egress controls for predictable carrier allow-lists
Consider zone-redundant storage for inbound file staging
securityBaselineHints: Disable legacy TLS on all public-facing controls before cutover
Encrypt backups with CMK when handling regulated health metadata
